name: Mediterranean Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 cups dried beans, soaked and drained
1/4 cup Italian parsley (plus more for garnish), chopped
freshly ground black pepper - to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy 3-quart stockpot or soup kettle, heat the extra-virgin olive oil.
2. Saute the onion, carrots, and garlic until the vegetables are soft but not browned, about 10-15 minutes.
3. Add the drained beans and boiling water to the pot. Stir in the thyme, bay leaf, and parsley.
4. Cover and cook over low heat, adding more boiling water if necessary, for 1 1/2 - 3 hours, or until the beans are soft (cooking time varies with the age of the beans).
5. When the beans are soft, add salt and pepper to taste.
6. For a thicker soup, remove about 1 1/2 cups of beans, puree in a food processor or blender, and return to the pot. For a thinner soup, add hot water (or crushed canned tomatoes with juices).
7. Correct seasonings as needed.
8. Transfer the soup to a tureen or individual bowls.
9. Garnish with chopped parsley or croutons and drizzle with a good green olive oil.
Makes 8 servings.
Notes:
This recipe can be adapted to almost any kind of bean (cannellini, Great Northern, anasazi, pinto, pinquito, etc.). From an article on the Mediterranean diet in the March-April 1995 issue of Modern Maturity magazine. Uploaded to the CompuServe Cooks Library on 5/13/96 by Lon Hall.
